title: SigmaSeparationEquivalences
lean_code_with_comments:
theorem sigma_separation_iff_all_walks_blocked
    (G : CDMG Node) (A B C : Set Node)
    (hA : A ⊆ ↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V) (hB : B ⊆ ↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V) (hC : C ⊆ ↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V) :
    G.IsISigmaSeparated A B C hA hB hC ↔
      ∀ {u v : Node} (π : Walk G u v),
          u ∈ A → v ∈ (G.J : Set Node) ∪ B → π.IsSigmaBlockedGiven C hC
-- claim_3_24 -- end statement
:= Iff.rfl

theorem sigma_separation_iff_all_paths_blocked
    (G : CDMG Node) (A B C : Set Node)
    (hA : A ⊆ ↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V) (hB : B ⊆ ↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V) (hC : C ⊆ ↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V) :
    G.IsISigmaSeparated A B C hA hB hC ↔
      ∀ {u v : Node} (π : Walk G u v),
          π.IsPath → u ∈ A → v ∈ (G.J : Set Node) ∪ B →
          π.IsSigmaBlockedGiven C hC
-- claim_3_24 -- end statement
:= by
  constructor
  · -- (⇒): every walk blocked ⇒ every path blocked.
    intro h u v π _ hu hv
    exact h π hu hv
  · -- (⇐): every path blocked ⇒ every walk blocked.  Contradiction via
    -- the open-walk-to-open-path lift of claim_3_23.
    intro h_paths
    rw [sigma_separation_iff_all_walks_blocked]
    intro u v π hu hv
    by_contra h_nb
    have h_open : π.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC := by
      refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
      · intro k vk hvk hcol
        by_contra h_nin
        exact h_nb (Or.inl ⟨k, vk, hvk, hcol, h_nin⟩)
      · intro k vk hvk hbnc
        by_contra h_in
        exact h_nb (Or.inr ⟨k, vk, hvk, hbnc, h_in⟩)
    have hu_mem : u ∈ (↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V : Set Node) := hA hu
    have hv_mem : v ∈ (↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V : Set Node) := by
      rcases hv with h | h
      · exact Or.inl h
      · exact hB h
    have h_tfae := sigma_open_paths_walks G C hC hu_mem hv_mem
    have h_ex_walk : ∃ π' : Walk G u v, π'.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC := ⟨π, h_open⟩
    obtain ⟨π_p, h_path, h_open_p⟩ := (h_tfae.out 1 0).mp h_ex_walk
    have h_blocked_p : π_p.IsSigmaBlockedGiven C hC := h_paths π_p h_path hu hv
    rcases h_blocked_p with ⟨k, vk, hvk, hcol, hnin⟩ | ⟨k, vk, hvk, hbnc, hin⟩
    · exact hnin (h_open_p.1 k vk hvk hcol)
    · exact (h_open_p.2 k vk hvk hbnc) hin

theorem exists_shortest_sigma_open_path
    (G : CDMG Node) (A B C : Set Node)
    (hA : A ⊆ ↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V) (hB : B ⊆ ↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V) (hC : C ⊆ ↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V)
    (h : G.IsNotISigmaSeparated A B C hA hB hC) :
    ∃ (u v : Node) (π : Walk G u v),
        u ∈ A ∧
        v ∈ (G.J : Set Node) ∪ B ∧
        π.IsPath ∧
        π.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC ∧
        (∀ (u' v' : Node) (π' : Walk G u' v'),
            u' ∈ A → v' ∈ (G.J : Set Node) ∪ B →
            π'.IsPath → π'.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC →
            π.length ≤ π'.length)
-- claim_3_24 -- end statement
:= by
  -- Step 1: Extract a σ-open walk witness from h : IsNotISigmaSeparated.
  have h_exists_walk : ∃ (u v : Node) (π : Walk G u v),
      u ∈ A ∧ v ∈ (G.J : Set Node) ∪ B ∧ π.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC := by
    by_contra h_ne
    apply h
    rw [sigma_separation_iff_all_walks_blocked]
    intro u v π hu hv
    by_contra h_nb
    apply h_ne
    refine ⟨u, v, π, hu, hv, ?_, ?_⟩
    · intro k vk hvk hcol
      by_contra h_nin
      exact h_nb (Or.inl ⟨k, vk, hvk, hcol, h_nin⟩)
    · intro k vk hvk hbnc
      by_contra h_in
      exact h_nb (Or.inr ⟨k, vk, hvk, hbnc, h_in⟩)
  obtain ⟨u₀, v₀, π₀, hu₀, hv₀, h_open_w⟩ := h_exists_walk
  -- Step 2: endpoint memberships for claim_3_23.
  have hu₀_mem : u₀ ∈ (↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V : Set Node) := hA hu₀
  have hv₀_mem : v₀ ∈ (↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V : Set Node) := by
    rcases hv₀ with h | h
    · exact Or.inl h
    · exact hB h
  -- Step 3: apply claim_3_23 (2)↔(1) to extract a σ-open path.
  have h_tfae := sigma_open_paths_walks G C hC hu₀_mem hv₀_mem
  have h_ex_w : ∃ π' : Walk G u₀ v₀, π'.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC := ⟨π₀, h_open_w⟩
  obtain ⟨π_p, h_path, h_open_p⟩ := (h_tfae.out 1 0).mp h_ex_w
  -- Step 4: pick the minimum length via Nat.find.
  classical
  let P : ℕ → Prop := fun m =>
    ∃ (u v : Node) (π : Walk G u v),
      u ∈ A ∧ v ∈ (G.J : Set Node) ∪ B ∧
      π.IsPath ∧ π.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC ∧ π.length = m
  have h_ne : ∃ m, P m :=
    ⟨π_p.length, u₀, v₀, π_p, hu₀, hv₀, h_path, h_open_p, rfl⟩
  obtain ⟨u_s, v_s, π_s, hu_s, hv_s, hpath_s, hopen_s, h_len_s⟩ :
      P (Nat.find h_ne) := Nat.find_spec h_ne
  refine ⟨u_s, v_s, π_s, hu_s, hv_s, hpath_s, hopen_s, ?_⟩
  intro u' v' π' hu' hv' hpath' hopen'
  have h_pl : P π'.length :=
    ⟨u', v', π', hu', hv', hpath', hopen', rfl⟩
  have h_le : Nat.find h_ne ≤ π'.length := Nat.find_le h_pl
  rw [h_len_s]
  exact h_le

theorem exists_shortest_sigma_open_walk_colliders_in_C
    (G : CDMG Node) (A B C : Set Node)
    (hA : A ⊆ ↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V) (hB : B ⊆ ↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V) (hC : C ⊆ ↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V)
    (h : G.IsNotISigmaSeparated A B C hA hB hC) :
    ∃ (u v : Node) (π : Walk G u v),
        u ∈ A ∧
        v ∈ (G.J : Set Node) ∪ B ∧
        π.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC ∧
        (∀ (k : ℕ) (vk : Node),
            π.vertices[k]? = some vk → π.IsCollider k → vk ∈ C) ∧
        (∀ (u' v' : Node) (π' : Walk G u' v'),
            u' ∈ A → v' ∈ (G.J : Set Node) ∪ B →
            π'.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC →
            (∀ (k : ℕ) (vk : Node),
                π'.vertices[k]? = some vk → π'.IsCollider k → vk ∈ C) →
            π.length ≤ π'.length)
-- claim_3_24 -- end statement
:= by
  -- Step 1: Extract a σ-open walk witness from h : IsNotISigmaSeparated.
  have h_exists_walk : ∃ (u v : Node) (π : Walk G u v),
      u ∈ A ∧ v ∈ (G.J : Set Node) ∪ B ∧ π.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC := by
    by_contra h_ne
    apply h
    rw [sigma_separation_iff_all_walks_blocked]
    intro u v π hu hv
    by_contra h_nb
    apply h_ne
    refine ⟨u, v, π, hu, hv, ?_, ?_⟩
    · intro k vk hvk hcol
      by_contra h_nin
      exact h_nb (Or.inl ⟨k, vk, hvk, hcol, h_nin⟩)
    · intro k vk hvk hbnc
      by_contra h_in
      exact h_nb (Or.inr ⟨k, vk, hvk, hbnc, h_in⟩)
  obtain ⟨u₀, v₀, π₀, hu₀, hv₀, h_open_w⟩ := h_exists_walk
  -- Step 2: endpoint memberships for claim_3_23.
  have hu₀_mem : u₀ ∈ (↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V : Set Node) := hA hu₀
  have hv₀_mem : v₀ ∈ (↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V : Set Node) := by
    rcases hv₀ with h | h
    · exact Or.inl h
    · exact hB h
  -- Step 3: apply claim_3_23 (2)↔(3) to extract a σ-open walk with all
  -- colliders in C.
  have h_tfae := sigma_open_paths_walks G C hC hu₀_mem hv₀_mem
  have h_ex_w : ∃ π' : Walk G u₀ v₀, π'.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC := ⟨π₀, h_open_w⟩
  obtain ⟨π_w, h_open_πw, h_colliders_C⟩ := (h_tfae.out 1 2).mp h_ex_w
  -- Step 4: pick the minimum length via Nat.find.
  classical
  let P : ℕ → Prop := fun m =>
    ∃ (u v : Node) (π : Walk G u v),
      u ∈ A ∧ v ∈ (G.J : Set Node) ∪ B ∧
      π.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC ∧
      (∀ (k : ℕ) (vk : Node),
          π.vertices[k]? = some vk → π.IsCollider k → vk ∈ C) ∧
      π.length = m
  have h_ne : ∃ m, P m :=
    ⟨π_w.length, u₀, v₀, π_w, hu₀, hv₀, h_open_πw, h_colliders_C, rfl⟩
  obtain ⟨u_s, v_s, π_s, hu_s, hv_s, hopen_s, hcolc_s, h_len_s⟩ :
      P (Nat.find h_ne) := Nat.find_spec h_ne
  refine ⟨u_s, v_s, π_s, hu_s, hv_s, hopen_s, hcolc_s, ?_⟩
  intro u' v' π' hu' hv' hopen' hcolc'
  have h_pl : P π'.length :=
    ⟨u', v', π', hu', hv', hopen', hcolc', rfl⟩
  have h_le : Nat.find h_ne ≤ π'.length := Nat.find_le h_pl
  rw [h_len_s]
  exact h_le

    have h_blocked_p : π_p.IsSigmaBlockedGiven C hC := h_paths π_p h_path hu hv

theorem exists_shortest_sigma_open_path

    (hA : A ⊆ ↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V) (hB : B ⊆ ↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V) (hC : C ⊆ ↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V)
    (h : G.IsNotISigmaSeparated A B C hA hB hC) :
    ∃ (u v : Node) (π : Walk G u v),
        u ∈ A ∧
        v ∈ (G.J : Set Node) ∪ B ∧
        π.IsPath ∧
        π.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC ∧

            u' ∈ A → v' ∈ (G.J : Set Node) ∪ B →
            π'.IsPath → π'.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC →

  have h_exists_walk : ∃ (u v : Node) (π : Walk G u v),

    · intro k vk hvk hcol

  have hv₀_mem : v₀ ∈ (↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V : Set Node) := by

    · exact Or.inl h
    · exact hB h

  have h_tfae := sigma_open_paths_walks G C hC hu₀_mem hv₀_mem
  have h_ex_w : ∃ π' : Walk G u₀ v₀, π'.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC := ⟨π₀, h_open_w⟩
  obtain ⟨π_p, h_path, h_open_p⟩ := (h_tfae.out 1 0).mp h_ex_w

  classical
  let P : ℕ → Prop := fun m =>
    ∃ (u v : Node) (π : Walk G u v),
      u ∈ A ∧ v ∈ (G.J : Set Node) ∪ B ∧
      π.IsPath ∧ π.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC ∧ π.length = m

  rw [h_len_s]
  exact h_le

theorem exists_shortest_sigma_open_walk_colliders_in_C
    (G : CDMG Node) (A B C : Set Node)
    (hA : A ⊆ ↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V) (hB : B ⊆ ↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V) (hC : C ⊆ ↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V)
    (h : G.IsNotISigmaSeparated A B C hA hB hC) :
    ∃ (u v : Node) (π : Walk G u v),
        u ∈ A ∧
        v ∈ (G.J : Set Node) ∪ B ∧
        π.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC ∧
        (∀ (k : ℕ) (vk : Node),
            π.vertices[k]? = some vk → π.IsCollider k → vk ∈ C) ∧
        (∀ (u' v' : Node) (π' : Walk G u' v'),
            u' ∈ A → v' ∈ (G.J : Set Node) ∪ B →
            π'.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC →
            (∀ (k : ℕ) (vk : Node),
                π'.vertices[k]? = some vk → π'.IsCollider k → vk ∈ C) →
            π.length ≤ π'.length)

:= by

  have h_exists_walk : ∃ (u v : Node) (π : Walk G u v),
      u ∈ A ∧ v ∈ (G.J : Set Node) ∪ B ∧ π.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC := by
    by_contra h_ne
    apply h
    rw [sigma_separation_iff_all_walks_blocked]
    intro u v π hu hv
    by_contra h_nb
    apply h_ne
    refine ⟨u, v, π, hu, hv, ?_, ?_⟩
    · intro k vk hvk hcol
      by_contra h_nin
      exact h_nb (Or.inl ⟨k, vk, hvk, hcol, h_nin⟩)
    · intro k vk hvk hbnc
      by_contra h_in
      exact h_nb (Or.inr ⟨k, vk, hvk, hbnc, h_in⟩)
  obtain ⟨u₀, v₀, π₀, hu₀, hv₀, h_open_w⟩ := h_exists_walk

  have hu₀_mem : u₀ ∈ (↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V : Set Node) := hA hu₀
  have hv₀_mem : v₀ ∈ (↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V : Set Node) := by
    rcases hv₀ with h | h
    · exact Or.inl h
    · exact hB h

  have h_tfae := sigma_open_paths_walks G C hC hu₀_mem hv₀_mem
  have h_ex_w : ∃ π' : Walk G u₀ v₀, π'.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC := ⟨π₀, h_open_w⟩
  obtain ⟨π_w, h_open_πw, h_colliders_C⟩ := (h_tfae.out 1 2).mp h_ex_w

  classical
  let P : ℕ → Prop := fun m =>
    ∃ (u v : Node) (π : Walk G u v),
      u ∈ A ∧ v ∈ (G.J : Set Node) ∪ B ∧
      π.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC ∧
      (∀ (k : ℕ) (vk : Node),
          π.vertices[k]? = some vk → π.IsCollider k → vk ∈ C) ∧
      π.length = m
  have h_ne : ∃ m, P m :=
    ⟨π_w.length, u₀, v₀, π_w, hu₀, hv₀, h_open_πw, h_colliders_C, rfl⟩
  obtain ⟨u_s, v_s, π_s, hu_s, hv_s, hopen_s, hcolc_s, h_len_s⟩ :
      P (Nat.find h_ne) := Nat.find_spec h_ne
  refine ⟨u_s, v_s, π_s, hu_s, hv_s, hopen_s, hcolc_s, ?_⟩
  intro u' v' π' hu' hv' hopen' hcolc'
  have h_pl : P π'.length :=
    ⟨u', v', π', hu', hv', hopen', hcolc', rfl⟩
  have h_le : Nat.find h_ne ≤ π'.length := Nat.find_le h_pl
  rw [h_len_s]
  exact h_le
lean_explanation:
The file formalises four corollaries of `claim_3_23` (the three-way TFAE between $C$-$\sigma$-open paths, $C$-$\sigma$-open walks, and $C$-$\sigma$-open walks whose colliders all lie in $C$). Each LN sub-item is recorded as its own Lean theorem so downstream consumers can cite the parts independently.

The ambient binder block is `{Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]` — the chapter-wide convention used by every `CDMG`-opening file in Section~3. `G : CDMG Node` is a conditional directed mixed graph (`def_3_15`), and `↑G.J`, `↑G.V` are the input-node and internal-node sets coerced to `Set Node`. `A B C : Set Node` are the three node sets of the LN; each carries a subset hypothesis `hA, hB, hC : · ⊆ ↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V` matching the LN setup. The asymmetric right-endpoint set `(G.J : Set Node) ∪ B` is the LN's deliberate `J`-inclusion from `def_3_18`, not a typo.

A few Mathlib idioms are worth flagging. `Walk G u v` is the bespoke inductive type from Section~3.1 describing a walk in `G` from `u` to `v`; `π.IsPath`, `π.IsSigmaOpenGiven C hC`, `π.IsSigmaBlockedGiven C hC` are predicates on it. `π.IsCollider k` says position `k` on `π` is a collider in the sense $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) = 2$. The `set_option linter.unusedVariables false in` directive silences a spurious warning since `hA` appears only in the type. The minimisation in parts (2a) and (2b) uses Mathlib's `Nat.find` — picking the least `m` satisfying a decidable predicate — as the constructive avatar of well-ordering of $\mathbb{N}$.

The theorems split into two equivalences and two existentials. Parts (1a) and (1b) are `Iff`s: $A \perp^{i\sigma}_G B \mid C$ iff every walk (resp. path) from a node in `A` to a node in `J \cup B` is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked. Parts (2a) and (2b) take `IsNotISigmaSeparated` as hypothesis and conclude an existence of a length-minimum witness: a shortest $C$-$\sigma$-open path for (2a), and a shortest $C$-$\sigma$-open walk with every collider lying strictly in `C` for (2b). The endpoints `u, v` are existentialised alongside `π : Walk G u v` in a flat `∃`, and the `(shortest)` qualifier is encoded inline as a universal bound `∀ π', cond π' → π.length ≤ π'.length` over the same conditioned class as the witness.
design_choices:
**Four separate theorems, not one bundled conjunction.** Each LN sub-item — (1a), (1b), (2a), (2b) — is its own `theorem`. Bundling them into a single `And`-shaped conclusion was considered and rejected: downstream consumers cite the sub-items in isolation (1a/1b for the $\sigma$-separation characterisations; 2a/2b for the $\sigma$-non-separation existence witnesses), and a single bundled lemma would force every caller through a nested tuple projection.

**Part (1a) is recorded as a separate `Iff` even though its right-hand side is a definitional unfolding.** The body of (1a) is literally the body of `IsISigmaSeparated`, so the proof is `Iff.rfl`. The LN nevertheless lists (1a) and (1b) on equal footing as two equivalent characterisations; recording (1a) as a named `Iff` preserves that symmetric citation pattern, so downstream code never has to know that one direction is a def-unfold and the other a substantive translation.

**Part (1b)'s $(\Leftarrow)$ direction routes through `claim_3_23`.** Going from "every path is blocked" to "every walk is blocked" needs the $\sigma$-open-walk $\to$ $\sigma$-open-path lift: a counterexample walk yields a $\sigma$-open *path* with the same endpoints via `claim_3_23 (2)↔(1)`, contradicting the universal-path hypothesis. The $(\Rightarrow)$ direction is trivial (a path is a walk).

**The `(shortest)` qualifier is encoded inline as a universal bound over the *joint* class.** Rather than wrap the witness in a `MinLengthWitness` structure, the existential writes `∀ π', cond π' → π.length ≤ π'.length` directly, with `cond π'` being the *same* conjunction (start in `A`, end in `J ∪ B`, $\sigma$-open, etc.) that the witness itself satisfies. The minimum is therefore taken within the joint class, not within the wider class of $\sigma$-open walks alone. The competing reading — "the literal shortest path between `A` and `J ∪ B` irrespective of $\sigma$-openness is itself $\sigma$-open" — was considered and rejected as both textually unsupported and false in general.

**Part (2b)'s collider clause is a strict strengthening of $\sigma$-openness, not a derivable consequence.** $C$-$\sigma$-openness only forces colliders to lie in $\mathrm{Anc}^G(C)$; (2b) demands they lie in `C` itself. The encoding writes this as an explicit second conjunct on the witness predicate and on the minimisation class, mirroring `claim_3_23`'s variant (3). No `Walk.AllCollidersIn` helper was extracted: the predicate is a single-line universal whose body is identical to the third clause of `claim_3_23`'s TFAE, so introducing a named helper would obscure the LN-faithful surface without saving any keystrokes downstream.

**Endpoints existentialised at the statement surface, not fixed by an outer universal.** The LN reads "there exists a walk from a node in `A` to a node in `J ∪ B`", so the start and end nodes are existential witnesses. The encoding existentialises `u, v : Node` alongside the walk in a flat `∃` — no sigma-types, no `Classical.choose`-style picked witness — to keep the plain-English reading visible at the type level.
